core_mechanism: Reduction of a governing differential system by exploiting its invariance under a continuous (Lie point) symmetry group: the symmetry generators (here an infinite-dimensional algebra parametrized by arbitrary functions) are used to construct group-invariant reductions that lower the number of independent variables, after which the movable-singularity (Painleve) structure of the reduced equation certifies integrability.
brief_reason: The abstract derives point symmetries forming an infinite-dimensional Lie algebra with arbitrary functions, substitutes these into the general symmetry vectors to drive the reduction, and then applies singularity (Painleve) analysis to the resulting lower-order equation as the integrability test.
